criteria.name: Data Completeness
criteria.1: Missing one or more financial statements, key line items absent, no supplemental data (R&D history, lease schedule)
criteria.3: All three statements collected with major line items, some supplemental data present, at least one year of history
criteria.5: All three statements with complete line items for 2+ years, R&D history for N+1 years, lease commitment schedule, effective and marginal tax rates documented, SBC identified

criteria.name: R&D Capitalization
criteria.1: R&D not considered for capitalization despite being material, or capitalized with no justification for asset life, amortization schedule missing
criteria.3: R&D capitalized with reasonable asset life, amortization schedule computed, operating income adjusted, but minor errors or incomplete documentation
criteria.5: R&D capitalized with industry-appropriate asset life justified, complete amortization schedule showing each prior year, research asset computed, operating income and balance sheet both adjusted, tax effect accounted for

criteria.name: Operating Lease Conversion
criteria.1: Operating leases ignored despite material commitments, or converted without discounting, or wrong discount rate used
criteria.3: Lease commitments discounted at a reasonable rate, debt equivalent computed, balance sheet adjusted, but income statement adjustment incomplete or discount rate not justified
criteria.5: Lease commitments discounted at pre-tax cost of debt with rate justified, PV computed year by year including treatment of lump-sum 'thereafter' amount, both balance sheet and income statement adjusted (operating expense reclassified to depreciation plus interest), ASC 842 status checked

criteria.name: SBC Treatment
criteria.1: SBC added back to earnings or cash flow as if it were not a real cost, or SBC not mentioned despite being material
criteria.3: SBC treated as operating expense and not added back, magnitude noted relative to revenue or operating income
criteria.5: SBC treated as real operating expense, not added back in any adjusted metric, magnitude quantified and flagged if above 10% of revenue, diluted share count or option value adjustment applied to per-share value

criteria.name: Normalization Quality
criteria.1: One-time items not identified, or recurring charges excluded as 'non-recurring' without justification, or cyclical effects ignored
criteria.3: Major one-time items identified and removed, some justification provided for classification, cyclical effects acknowledged
criteria.5: All significant non-recurring items identified with specific justification for each, recurring 'non-recurring' charges recognized and averaged rather than excluded, cyclical normalization applied where appropriate, before/after impact documented for each item

criteria.name: FCFF Accuracy
criteria.1: FCFF formula incorrect or major components missing, tax rate wrong, CapEx or depreciation not adjusted for R&D capitalization
criteria.3: FCFF formula correct, major components present, tax rate reasonable, but minor inconsistencies (e.g., using effective instead of marginal tax rate, or missing working capital adjustment)
criteria.5: FCFF computed from adjusted operating income using marginal tax rate, CapEx and depreciation consistent with R&D capitalization treatment, working capital change computed correctly excluding cash and debt, all components documented

criteria.name: FCFE Accuracy
criteria.1: FCFE not computed, or formula incorrect, or does not reconcile with FCFF
criteria.3: FCFE computed with correct formula, net debt change included, reconciliation with FCFF attempted
criteria.5: FCFE computed correctly, reconciles with FCFF within rounding tolerance (FCFE = FCFF - after-tax interest + net debt change), discrepancies investigated and explained if present

criteria.name: Ratio Calculation
criteria.1: Ratios computed from unadjusted figures, key ratios missing, or formula errors in computation
criteria.3: Major ratios computed from adjusted figures (ROIC, ROE, operating margin, debt-to-capital), most formulas correct
criteria.5: Comprehensive ratio dashboard computed from adjusted figures covering profitability (ROIC, ROE, margins), leverage (debt-to-capital, coverage), and efficiency (reinvestment rate, sales-to-capital), multi-year trends shown, all formulas correct

criteria.name: Industry Comparison
criteria.1: No industry context provided, ratios presented in isolation with no benchmarks
criteria.3: Company ratios compared to industry medians for key metrics, sector identified, some context for outlier ratios
criteria.5: Company ratios placed in industry distribution (quartiles or percentiles), outliers flagged and explained, comparison covers profitability, leverage, and efficiency metrics, source of benchmarks noted

criteria.name: Adjustment Documentation
criteria.1: Adjustments applied without explanation, before/after values not shown, rationale absent
criteria.3: Most adjustments documented with before/after values and brief rationale, key assumptions stated
criteria.5: Every adjustment documented with pre-adjustment value, post-adjustment value, rationale, key assumptions (asset life, discount rate, tax rate), and impact on EBIT, invested capital, and FCFF. Summary table showing all adjustments and their cumulative effect

key_requirements: R&D capitalization is the critical adjustment -- collect full R&D history, use 2-3 year asset life for software or 5-10 for pharma/biotech
SBC treatment is especially important -- tech companies often have SBC exceeding 10% of revenue
CapEx and depreciation in FCFF must be adjusted to include R&D capitalization and R&D amortization
Research asset materially increases invested capital, which typically reduces ROIC below the reported figure
common_pitfalls: Adding back SBC to compute 'adjusted' cash flow (this overstates true cash generation)
Using wrong asset life for R&D (too long inflates research asset, too short understates it)
Not adjusting both operating income and invested capital (adjusting one without the other distorts ROIC)

key_requirements: Operating lease conversion is the critical adjustment -- compute PV of all commitments at pre-tax cost of debt
Verify whether ASC 842 / IFRS 16 has already put leases on the balance sheet before applying conversion
Separate maintenance CapEx (sustaining current operations) from growth CapEx (expanding) if possible
Depreciation analysis: check if accelerated depreciation distorts operating income relative to economic reality
common_pitfalls: Double-counting leases that are already on the balance sheet under ASC 842
Using WACC instead of pre-tax cost of debt to discount lease commitments
Ignoring the 'thereafter' lump sum or discounting it incorrectly

key_requirements: Do not compute FCFF -- debt is operational for banks and insurance companies, not financial
Focus on equity metrics: ROE, book value, excess return (ROE - cost of equity)
Provision for credit losses is a key operating expense that requires careful analysis
Regulatory capital requirements constrain the equity base
common_pitfalls: Treating debt as financial and subtracting it from firm value (debt is the raw material for financial firms)
Computing ROIC (meaningless when debt is operational)
Ignoring regulatory capital requirements when assessing available equity

key_requirements: Normalize earnings across a full business cycle (5-10 years), not just the most recent year
Identify whether the company is at a cyclical peak, trough, or mid-cycle
Restructuring charges that recur with each downturn are part of the cycle, not one-time items
Working capital swings can be large for cyclical firms -- use average change, not a single year
common_pitfalls: Using peak-year earnings as 'normal' (leads to overvaluation at cycle tops)
Excluding restructuring charges that appear in most downturns
Not recognizing that margins revert toward industry averages over a full cycle

common_failure_modes.symptom: R&D capitalized in operating income but CapEx not adjusted, or lease converted on balance sheet but operating income not adjusted
common_failure_modes.detection: ROIC changes dramatically from one adjustment but FCFF barely changes (or vice versa), suggesting incomplete propagation
common_failure_modes.fix: Trace each adjustment through all three financial statements: income statement, balance sheet, and cash flow. Verify consistency.

common_failure_modes.symptom: Using effective tax rate (which may include one-time items, deferred taxes, or foreign rate differences) instead of marginal rate for forward-looking FCFF
common_failure_modes.detection: FCFF tax rate diverges significantly from statutory rate without explanation
common_failure_modes.fix: Use marginal tax rate for FCFF computation. Document the rate used and explain any divergence from the statutory rate.

common_failure_modes.symptom: FCFE computed independently does not equal FCFF minus after-tax interest plus net debt change
common_failure_modes.detection: Compute both independently and check the reconciliation formula
common_failure_modes.fix: Identify the missing item. Common culprits: preferred dividends, minority interest payments, non-operating income included in one but not the other.

common_failure_modes.symptom: Cash flow or earnings figures labeled 'adjusted' that add back stock-based compensation
common_failure_modes.detection: Compare reported GAAP figures to adjusted figures; if SBC is added back, flag it
common_failure_modes.fix: Treat SBC as a real operating expense. If the analysis requires a cash-only view, label it explicitly and note that it overstates economic earnings.

common_failure_modes.symptom: Restructuring charges, goodwill impairments, or integration costs excluded in multiple consecutive years
common_failure_modes.detection: Review five years of income statements. If the same type of 'non-recurring' item appears in three or more years, it is recurring.
common_failure_modes.fix: Average the item over the period rather than excluding it. Only exclude items that are genuinely tied to specific, non-repeating events.

common_failure_modes.symptom: Cash included in invested capital (inflating the denominator for ROIC), or interest income included in EBIT (inflating the numerator)
common_failure_modes.detection: ROIC is unusually low (cash in denominator) or unusually high (non-operating income in numerator)
common_failure_modes.fix: Separate operating items (used in business) from non-operating items (financial assets, excess cash). Compute ROIC using only operating items.

common_failure_modes.symptom: FCFF computed as NOPAT minus net CapEx with no working capital change
common_failure_modes.detection: FCFF is suspiciously close to NOPAT minus net CapEx
common_failure_modes.fix: Compute change in non-cash working capital (non-cash current assets minus non-debt current liabilities) and subtract from FCFF.

common_failure_modes.symptom: ROIC, operating margin, or debt-to-capital computed from as-reported financials despite material adjustments being made
common_failure_modes.detection: Ratios do not reflect adjustments documented elsewhere in the analysis
common_failure_modes.fix: Compute all ratios from the adjusted financial statements. Show both reported and adjusted ratios for transparency.
